feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: PhD student at Tsinghua University. Researching Physical Organic Chemistry with a focus on **Radical Kinetics**, **Photocatalysis**, and **Time-resolved EPR Spectroscopy**.

entry.title: Selected for the 74th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting

link.title: Selected for the 74th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting

entry.content:
I am honored to have been selected as one of the 30 Chinese PhD students to participate in the 74th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting (Chemistry). This globally acclaimed forum connects Nobel Laureates with emerging scientists to foster cross-generational inspiration.

Highlights of the Experience:

  • German University Tour: Prior to the meeting, I joined the Chinese delegation for an insightful tour of the University of Bonn, University of Giessen, University of Bayreuth, and the Technical University of Munich. I specifically audited the scientific infrastructure and laboratory management models at these institutions.
  • Scientific Exchange: During the meeting, I engaged with multiple Nobel Laureates. A notable highlight was participating in an on-stage “arm-wrestling” demonstration with Professor Ben Feringa, which vividly illustrated the mechanical properties of molecular machines.

This opportunity provided unparalleled perspectives on global scientific trends and strengthened my commitment to rigorous research in physical organic chemistry.
entry.summary: Participation in the prestigious 74th Lindau Nobel Laureate Meeting and a scientific tour across Germany.

entry.title: Oral Presentation Prize at ACS Spring 2025

link.title: Oral Presentation Prize at ACS Spring 2025

entry.content:
I participated in the ACS Spring 2025 virtual meeting, specifically the Virtual Graduate Students Symposium in Asia-Pacific Region on Synthetic Chemistry session.

Presentation Details:

  • Topic: Overestimated halogen atom transfer reactivity of α-aminoalkyl radicals.
  • Core Discussion: The presentation focused on the kinetic auditing of α-aminoalkyl radicals and the potential overestimation of their reactivity in XAT processes.

Following the session, I was honored to receive the Oral Presentation Prize. This recognition validates our group’s efforts in elucidating the fundamental kinetic behaviors of open-shell intermediates.
entry.summary: Presenting research on halogen atom transfer (XAT) reactivity at the ACS Spring 2025 Virtual Graduate Students Symposium.

entry.title: Outstanding Presentation Award at the 5th Beijing-Tianjin Organic Chemistry Symposium

link.title: Outstanding Presentation Award at the 5th Beijing-Tianjin Organic Chemistry Symposium

entry.content:
The 5th Beijing-Tianjin Graduate Student Organic Chemistry Symposium was held at Nankai University, bringing together approximately 300 students from Tsinghua University, Peking University, ICCAS, and Nankai University.

Key Achievements:

  • Oral Presentation: I delivered a talk regarding my recent progress in the study of transient intermediates. The work emphasized the importance of precise kinetic measurements in understanding complex reaction mechanisms.
  • Recognition: Based on the voting by attending faculty members from the participating institutions, I was awarded the Outstanding Presentation Award.

This symposium served as an excellent platform for local academic exchange and allowed for a deep dive into the diverse research frontiers of the Beijing-Tianjin organic chemistry community.
entry.summary: Awarded for oral presentation at the regional organic chemistry symposium held at Nankai University.
